title: The ending Timeframe in EoE
wikitext:
[]: The ending Timeframe in EoE             Quote Post by Tails_night » Wed Jul 21, 2010 5:03 am Alrighty, While realizing End of Evangelion itself takes place in probably less than one day, I used to be under the impression that the ending was instantly after the events with the wood grave markers bulding themselves and all these other anime mysteries... but now after thinking about things in a different light, and thinking maybe Shinji put them up, and has been alone for a while after waking up to find and try to kill Asuka. So now I'm wondering what was your first impression of this scene and how do you look at the timeframe now?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Jul 21, 2010 10:52 am [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=2270[/url] This thread has been around for a while, was recently brought back to life, and should more or less cover what you're asking.[/img]

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 21, 2010 11:46 am First impressions (as asked for) are different to considered analysis as per http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=2270 I got an impression of time passes, but not really that the answer was meaningful, between each scene change towards the end. After all, when you're left with two damned souls, you have all eternity to play with.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Jul 21, 2010 7:46 pm My initial thoughts were that EoE made no sense. Than NAW linked me here.
